answer:
We have a \(5 \times 5\) grid. The conditions mean each row and each column is monochromatic (all chips in a row same color, all chips in a column same color), and the arrangement is maximal—no extra chip can be added without breaking this.

If a row has a chip of a certain color, then every column containing a chip in that row must have that same color throughout. This forces the pattern to be a "grid" of rows and columns of one color, and the complementary rows/columns of the other color. The maximal condition means we cannot add any chip, so either all rows are filled with one color and all columns with the other, or vice versa, leading to a "cross" shape.
